title: The circus parable — dad story, 4/18/2021
html:
TL;DR

A short parable Yahir captured from his dad on 2021-04-18. An 18-year-old shows up at a circus looking for work. The manager, ego-chuckling, asks if he can sweep or mop. The kid says "I can imitate birds." The manager scoffs — "I have tigers and elephants." Mid-sentence, the kid gets up and starts flying away.

The note is one paragraph, attributed: "-dad 4/18/2021".

Key quote

"Almost mid-sentence the 18 year old gets up and starts flying away."

Why it matters in the graph

  • A clean specimen of dad wisdom — the second confirmed dad-attributed lesson in the vault (the other: "Impulse decision will kill you" from 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream).
  • The latent potential parable reads as a self-message: at 18, Yahir was applying to retail jobs (2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist). The kid in the parable is him.
  • Echoes the fear of not doing framing — undersold capacity, no one asks the right question.

Open questions

  • Is there a transcript or recording? The note is too clean to be ad-libbed; sounds polished from prior tellings.
  • Does dad have other parables Yahir's captured but not flagged with "-dad"?

Wikilinks
